source_inputs: OpenClaw critique pasted by the maintainer on 2026-04-29, originally from 2026-04-26

workflow.selection_rule: Pick one ready task whose dependencies are done. Update status before editing and after verification.

tasks.title: Enforce singleton MemPalace MCP client per plugin/runtime load
tasks.problem: Real OpenClaw agent activity can still create multiple mempalace.mcp_server children under the gateway, making OpenClaw feel unresponsive and increasing CPU load.
plan: Audit McpClient, ProcessManager, and plugin register paths for repeated starts.
Add a module-level or process-level singleton guard keyed by pythonBin and plugin id.
Ensure repeated register/runtime loads share the existing MCP client or close superseded clients deterministically.
Preserve the long-lived gateway MCP process while keeping CLI/status probes short-lived.
acceptance: Repeated status, health, and agent probes do not leave more than one gateway-owned mempalace.mcp_server child.
Full test suite passes.
New tests cover repeated runtime load/start behavior.
tasks.agent_prompt: Fix remempalace MCP lifecycle so repeated plugin/runtime loads cannot create multiple long-lived MemPalace subprocesses. Keep normal gateway runtime persistent and status/CLI probes cleanly closed.
evidence: Started RT-001: MCP singleton/lifecycle guard.
Workspace verification: npm run build plus focused vitest for mcp-client, index, memory-runtime passed with 45 tests.
Installed plugin verification: npm run build plus focused vitest for mcp-client, index, memory-runtime passed with 45 tests in /home/YOU/.openclaw/plugins/remempalace.
Runtime verification after openclaw gateway restart: gateway health OK, probe reachable, one openclaw-gateway process and one gateway-owned mempalace.mcp_server child.

tasks.title: Add cleanup coverage for aborts, restarts, and agent errors
tasks.problem: OpenClaw restarts and failed model calls can happen while remempalace hooks are active. Cleanup must be deterministic even when agent runs fail or commands are interrupted.
plan: Add tests around ProcessManager stop semantics and pending MCP calls.
Ensure SIGTERM/close paths reject pending calls and clear timers.
Make cleanup idempotent if stop is called twice.
Capture behavior in TROUBLESHOOTING or runtime docs.
acceptance: No hanging timers or orphan MCP processes after simulated stop during pending call.
ProcessManager stop is idempotent.
Gateway restart leaves one active MCP child.
tasks.agent_prompt: Harden remempalace shutdown and abort paths so interrupted status probes, gateway restarts, and failed agent runs cannot leak MCP subprocesses or timers.
evidence: Started RT-002: cleanup coverage for aborts, restarts, and agent errors.
Workspace verification: npm run build plus focused process-manager/mcp-client/memory-runtime vitest passed with 52 tests.
Workspace verification: full npm test passed with 246 tests and 2 skipped.
Installed plugin verification: npm run build and full npm test passed with 246 tests and 2 skipped in /home/YOU/.openclaw/plugins/remempalace.
Runtime verification after openclaw gateway restart: gateway health OK, one openclaw-gateway process and one gateway-owned mempalace.mcp_server child.

tasks.title: Stop predicate explosion in structured extraction
tasks.problem: The extractor can turn free text into unstable predicates such as uses_for_testing_and_playwright_for_e2e, polluting the KG.
plan: Define a stable predicate vocabulary in code.
Map free-text variants onto stable predicates.
Keep richer details in object text or metadata instead of predicate names.
Reject or downscore facts whose predicate cannot be normalized.
acceptance: Extractor never emits predicates outside the controlled vocabulary unless explicitly marked experimental.
Examples like 'using vitest for testing and playwright for e2e' emit stable uses or used_for facts, not predicate explosions.
Tests cover compound tool/purpose sentences.

commands: npx vitest run tests/structured-extractor.test.ts tests/contradiction.test.ts tests/kg-invalidate.test.ts

tasks.agent_prompt: Constrain remempalace structured fact extraction to a stable predicate vocabulary and prevent free-text predicate explosion while preserving useful object details.
evidence: Started KG-001: controlled predicate vocabulary and compound extraction.
Workspace verification: npm run build plus focused KG vitest passed with 38 tests.
Workspace verification: full npm test passed with 226 tests and 2 skipped.
Installed plugin verification: npm run build and full npm test passed with 226 tests and 2 skipped in /home/YOU/.openclaw/plugins/remempalace.

tasks.title: Revisit cardinality defaults for is_a and decided_to
tasks.problem: The critique says is_a and decided_to are too aggressively single-cardinality. This can invalidate valid multi-role or multi-decision facts.
plan: Audit predicate cardinality map.
Change is_a and decided_to to list cardinality.
Introduce narrower predicates for true single-cardinality cases, such as job_title or default_tool.
Update contradiction tests for multiple roles and multiple project decisions.
acceptance: A person can have multiple is_a facts without invalidation.
A project/person can have multiple decided_to facts without invalidation.
Single-cardinality behavior remains for favorite/default/current predicates where appropriate.
tasks.agent_prompt: Update remempalace contradiction cardinality so is_a and decided_to no longer erase valid multi-valued facts. Keep strict invalidation for genuinely single-valued predicates.
evidence: Started KG-002: cardinality defaults for is_a and decided_to.
Workspace verification: npm run build plus focused contradiction/kg-invalidate vitest passed with 20 tests.
Workspace verification: full npm test passed with 229 tests and 2 skipped.
Installed plugin verification: npm run build and full npm test passed with 229 tests and 2 skipped in /home/YOU/.openclaw/plugins/remempalace.

tasks.title: Make negation policy explicit and consistent
tasks.problem: Negation is partly handled by regex side effects. Some negated statements are ignored while others may be downscored, making learning inconsistent.
plan: Choose policy: skip negated facts for now.
Document the policy in extractor code and tests.
Ensure all negated forms consistently produce no learned fact unless explicit negated-fact support is added later.
Add test cases for 'not X', 'no longer X', 'does not use X', and 'is not a manager'.
acceptance: Negated statements consistently skip KG learning.
No negated statement accidentally creates a positive fact.
Future explicit negated-fact support is tracked as a separate follow-up if desired.
tasks.agent_prompt: Make remempalace negation handling boring and consistent: skip negated facts entirely unless a later explicit negated fact model is designed.
evidence: Started KG-003: explicit skip policy for negated facts.
Workspace verification: npm run build plus focused structured-extractor/contradiction vitest passed with 37 tests.
Workspace verification: full npm test passed with 230 tests and 2 skipped.
Installed plugin verification: npm run build and full npm test passed with 230 tests and 2 skipped in /home/YOU/.openclaw/plugins/remempalace.

tasks.title: Add provenance and source weighting for learned facts
tasks.problem: Current learning is assistant-output heavy. Assistants can restate, speculate, summarize, and hallucinate; user-originated claims should be weighted higher.
plan: Capture source role for extracted candidate facts: user, assistant, system-derived.
Extract from user messages in addition to assistant outputs.
Apply stricter confidence thresholds to assistant-originated facts.
Include provenance in KG add source metadata where supported.
acceptance: User-originated facts can be learned.
Assistant-originated facts require higher confidence or stricter predicates.
Tests prove assistant speculation is not learned when below threshold.

commands: npx vitest run tests/index.test.ts tests/structured-extractor.test.ts tests/kg.test.ts

tasks.agent_prompt: Extend remempalace fact learning to include user-message provenance and source weighting. User claims should outrank assistant restatements.
evidence: Started KG-004: provenance/source weighting for learned facts.
Verified MemPalace KG add supports source_closet and used openclaw:user/openclaw:assistant as provenance values.

tasks.title: Add adversarial structured extraction test matrix
tasks.problem: The extractor passes targeted tests but real language edge cases can break it fast.
plan: Add table-driven tests for compound clauses.
Cover 'X and Y', 'X over Y', multiple roles, multiple tools and purposes, and negation.
Include examples from the OpenClaw critique verbatim.
acceptance: All critique examples have explicit tests.
Tests verify both accepted facts and intentionally skipped statements.
No test relies on snapshot ambiguity.

tasks.title: Tighten expected warning noise in tests
tasks.problem: Tests repeatedly print memoryRuntime.allowedReadRoots warnings. This hides real warning regressions and makes the suite feel less clean.
plan: Decide whether empty allowedReadRoots is expected in tests or should have a default test allowlist.
For expected warnings, spy/assert them in focused tests and suppress them elsewhere.
Keep production warning behavior if it is useful.
acceptance: Full test suite output is materially quieter.
There is still a test proving empty allowedReadRoots warns when intended.
No real warnings are swallowed globally.
tasks.agent_prompt: Reduce remempalace test warning noise without hiding production warnings. Assert expected warnings in focused tests and keep the full suite easier to scan.
evidence: Started TEST-001: tighten expected warning noise in tests.

tasks.title: Update README status and avoid stale precise test counts

Docs mention that /mnt/c plugin paths may be blocked by OpenClaw as world-writable.

tasks.problem: Running npm install in WSL changed package-lock optional dependency libc metadata, creating noisy diffs.
plan: Determine the npm version difference causing lockfile churn.
Decide whether to commit regenerated lockfile or pin/package-manager guidance.
Document recommended npm version if needed.
acceptance: Maintainers know whether package-lock changes are expected.
Install docs avoid accidental lockfile churn for routine testing.
No unexplained package-lock diff remains in release branch.

Confirmed npm 10.9.x rewrites package-lock optional dependency libc metadata without dependency version changes; /mnt/c checkout can also produce line-ending-only lockfile churn.

Documented npm ci preference, package-lock diff check, and restore guidance in CONTRIBUTING.

tasks.title: Share plugin state across repeated register() calls
tasks.problem: OpenClaw invokes plugin.register() multiple times per gateway process — once per registration mode (setup-only, setup-runtime, tool-discovery, discovery, full, cli-metadata; see loader-B-GXgDrk.js:3654-3692). McpClient.shared() already returns a singleton across register calls, but McpMemPalaceRepository, DiaryReconciler, search/KG caches, the init promise, and the health-probe state are constructed fresh per closure. Only one closure ever runs ensureInit; the others keep their persistenceState at the constructor default "unavailable" and serve agent-tool calls (notably remempalace_status) with stale state. Observed symptom: gateway log shows successful probe + replay ("diary replay: 199/201 succeeded") while /remempalace status simultaneously reports diary_persistent: no, last_replay: none, and caches: 0 entries. Recall, recent diary writes, and KG writes still work — they ride the shared McpClient — but the durable-replay/status surface is incoherent.
plan: Add a module-level RuntimeState container keyed by the shared McpClient (or pythonBin fingerprint). Construct McpMemPalaceRepository, DiaryReconciler, searchCache, kgCache, the init promise, and the lastHealthProbe* fields inside it.

Refactor register() to call getRuntimeState(mcp, cfg) instead of constructing inline, and rebind the per-closure services (RecallService, PromptInjectionService, StatusController, agent-tool factories) to that shared state.
Extend resetSharedForTests so test harnesses can clear both the McpClient cache and the new RuntimeState map.
Add a register-twice identity test: two register() calls return services that observe the same probe result.
Validate live: after a clean gateway restart and one before_prompt_build, /remempalace status reports diary_persistent: yes from any closure.
acceptance: Two register() calls produce one McpMemPalaceRepository instance, one DiaryReconciler instance, and a shared init promise.
After one gateway restart + one agent prompt, /remempalace status shows diary_persistent: yes, diary.state: persistent (or fallback-active if pending > 0), last_replay populated.
Test suite remains green; new identity test passes.
RT-001 invariant holds — exactly one mempalace.mcp_server child per gateway run.
TROUBLESHOOTING.md gains a 'multi-register state mismatch' section that documents the contract.

commands: journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway --since "5 minutes ago" | grep -c "MCP client started"

tasks.agent_prompt: Implement the shared-state fix described in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-13-multi-register-state-sharing.md. Extend the McpClient.shared singleton pattern to McpMemPalaceRepository, DiaryReconciler, the caches, the init promise, and lastHealthProbe* state. Verify with the listed commands and confirm /remempalace status flips to diary_persistent: yes after one gateway restart + one prompt.

tasks.title: Make diary persistence probe timeout configurable (cold-start fix)
tasks.problem: The diary persistence probe used a hardcoded 500ms timeout (DIARY_IO_TIMEOUT_MS) borrowed from the prompt-path read budget. Cold-start MemPalace needs ~1.5–2s for embedding-model load plus first ChromaDB write, so the probe always timed out on a fresh gateway restart. The adapter parked persistenceState at tool-present and the replay path — which is gated on canPersistDiary === "persistent" — never drained pending JSONL entries.
plan: Add diary.persistenceProbeTimeoutMs to the config schema (RemempalaceConfig, DEFAULT_CONFIG, openclaw.plugin.json) with a 3000ms default.

Thread the option through DiaryServiceOptions and DiaryReconcilerOptions; use it in both verifyDiaryPersistence call sites, keeping DIARY_IO_TIMEOUT_MS (500ms) as the fallback so the prompt-path read budget stays separate.
Document the new field in CONFIGURATION.md and add a TROUBLESHOOTING.md section describing the symptom signature.
acceptance: Default 3000ms is large enough to cover cold-start MemPalace on supported platforms.
Prompt-path diary reads keep the 500ms budget — the new field only governs the startup/replay probe.
Type-check, lint, and full test suite green.
Live: after gateway restart, journalctl shows replay drain instead of 'diary persistence unverified: state=tool-present'.
tasks.agent_prompt: Already implemented on 2026-05-13. See changed files for the diff.
evidence: Verified on the maintainer's install: gateway restart → MCP child up → probe completed inside 3000ms → 'diary replay: 199/201 succeeded, 0 failed' in journalctl.
npm run build: clean. npm run lint: clean. npm test: 593 passed / 6 skipped across 40 test files (was 590/6 — no regressions; new paths exercised by existing diary tests).
Field documented in CONFIGURATION.md `diary` table and TROUBLESHOOTING.md 'Diary persistence stuck at tool-present / fallback-active after a fresh start' section.
followups: RT-005 — even with this fix, multi-register state mismatch leaves the status display showing a stale `unavailable` while the live closure has `persistent`. Tracked separately.
